### Gtk.AboutDialog

Parent: `Window` ?? Implements: `Accessible`, `Buildable`, `ConstraintTarget`, `Native`, `Root`, `ShortcutManager` ?? GType: `GtkAboutDialog` ?? C type: `GtkAboutDialog`

The `GtkAboutDialog` offers a simple way to display information about a program. The shown information includes the programs' logo, name, copyright...

#### Constructors

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| new | `AboutDialog.new() -> Widget` | gtk_about_dialog_new |  | Creates a new `GtkAboutDialog`. |

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| add_credit_section | `AboutDialog.add_credit_section(section_name: utf8, people: utf8) -> none` | gtk_about_dialog_add_credit_section |  | Creates a new section in the "Credits" page. |
| get_artists | `AboutDialog.get_artists() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_artists |  | Returns the names of the artists which are displayed in the credits page. |
| get_authors | `AboutDialog.get_authors() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_authors |  | Returns the names of the authors which are displayed in the credits page. |
| get_comments | `AboutDialog.get_comments() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_comments |  | Returns the comments string. |
| get_copyright | `AboutDialog.get_copyright() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_copyright |  | Returns the copyright string. |
| get_documenters | `AboutDialog.get_documenters() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_documenters |  | Returns the name of the documenters which are displayed in the credits page. |
| get_license | `AboutDialog.get_license() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_license |  | Returns the license information. |
| get_license_type | `AboutDialog.get_license_type() -> License` | gtk_about_dialog_get_license_type |  | Retrieves the license type. |

| get_program_name | `AboutDialog.get_program_name() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_program_name |  | Returns the program name displayed in the about dialog. |
| get_system_information | `AboutDialog.get_system_information() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_system_information |  | Returns the system information that is shown in the about dialog. |
| get_translator_credits | `AboutDialog.get_translator_credits() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_translator_credits |  | Returns the translator credits string which is displayed in the credits page. |
| get_version | `AboutDialog.get_version() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_version |  | Returns the version string. |
| get_website | `AboutDialog.get_website() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_website |  | Returns the website URL. |
| get_website_label | `AboutDialog.get_website_label() -> utf8` | gtk_about_dialog_get_website_label |  | Returns the label used for the website link. |

| version | utf8 | read, write | The version of the program. |
| website | utf8 | read, write | The URL for the link to the website of the program. This should be a string starting with `http://` or `https://`. |
| website-label | utf8 | read, write | The label for the link to the website of the program. |
| wrap-license | gboolean | read, write | Whether to wrap the text in the license dialog. |

The `GtkBox` widget arranges child widgets into a single row or column. !An example GtkBox Whether it is a row or column depends on the value of it...

| reorder_child_after | `Box.reorder_child_after(child: Widget, sibling: Widget?) -> none` | gtk_box_reorder_child_after |  | Moves @child to the position after @sibling in the list of @box children. If @sibling is %NULL, move @child to the first position. |
| set_baseline_child | `Box.set_baseline_child(child: gint) -> none` | gtk_box_set_baseline_child | 4.12 | Sets the baseline child of a box. This affects only vertical boxes. |
| set_baseline_position | `Box.set_baseline_position(position: BaselinePosition) -> none` | gtk_box_set_baseline_position |  | Sets the baseline position of a box. This affects only horizontal boxes with at least one baseline aligned child. If there is more vertical space a... |
| set_homogeneous | `Box.set_homogeneous(homogeneous: gboolean) -> none` | gtk_box_set_homogeneous |  | Sets whether or not all children of @box are given equal space in the box. |
| set_spacing | `Box.set_spacing(spacing: gint) -> none` | gtk_box_set_spacing |  | Sets the number of pixels to place between children of @box. |

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

| baseline-position | BaselinePosition | read, write | The position of the baseline aligned widgets if extra space is available. |
| homogeneous | gboolean | read, write | Whether the children should all be the same size. |
| spacing | gint | read, write | The amount of space between children. |

| day | gint | read, write | The selected day (as a number between 1 and 31). |
| month | gint | read, write | The selected month (as a number between 0 and 11). This property gets initially set to the current month. |
| show-day-names | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether day names are displayed. |
| show-heading | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether a heading is displayed. |
| show-week-numbers | gboolean | read, write | Determines whether week numbers are displayed. |
| year | gint | read, write | The selected year. This property gets initially set to the current year. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| day-selected | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user selects a day. |
| next-month | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user switched to the next month. |
| next-year | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when user switched to the next year. |
| prev-month | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when the user switched to the previous month. |
| prev-year | `` | none | first |  | Emitted when user switched to the previous year. |

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |

| active-id | utf8 | read, write | The value of the ID column of the active row. |

| has-entry | gboolean | read, write, construct-only | Whether the combo box has an entry. |
| has-frame | gboolean | read, write | The `has-frame` property controls whether a frame is drawn around the entry. |
| id-column | gint | read, write | The model column that provides string IDs for the values in the model, if != -1. |
| model | TreeModel | read, write | The model from which the combo box takes its values. |
| popup-fixed-width | gboolean | read, write | Whether the popup's width should be a fixed width matching the allocated width of the combo box. |
| popup-shown | gboolean | read | Whether the combo boxes dropdown is popped up. Note that this property is mainly useful, because it allows you to connect to notify::popup-shown. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate | `` | none | first | 4.6 | Emitted to when the combo box is activated. The `::activate` signal on `GtkComboBox` is an action signal and emitting it causes the combo box to po... |
| changed | `` | none | last |  | Emitted when the active item is changed. The can be due to the user selecting a different item from the list, or due to a call to [method@Gtk.Combo... |
| format-entry-text | `path: utf8` | utf8 | last |  | Emitted to allow changing how the text in a combo box's entry is displayed. See [property@Gtk.ComboBox:has-entry]. Connect a signal handler which r... |
| move-active | `scroll_type: ScrollType` | none | last |  | Emitted to move the active selection. This is an keybinding signal. |
| popdown | `` | gboolean | last |  | Emitted to popdown the combo box list. This is an keybinding signal. The default bindings for this signal are Alt+Up and Escape. |
| popup | `` | none | last |  | Emitted to popup the combo box list. This is an keybinding signal. The default binding for this signal is Alt+Down. |

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |

| leave | `` | none | last |  | Emitted on the drop site when the pointer leaves the widget. Its main purpose it to undo things done in [signal@Gtk.DropTarget::enter]. |
| motion | `x: gdouble, y: gdouble` | Gdk.DragAction | last |  | Emitted while the pointer is moving over the drop target. |

| get_use_underline | `Expander.get_use_underline() -> gboolean` | gtk_expander_get_use_underline |  | Returns whether an underline in the text indicates a mnemonic. |

| set_expanded | `Expander.set_expanded(expanded: gboolean) -> none` | gtk_expander_set_expanded |  | Sets the state of the expander. Set to %TRUE, if you want the child widget to be revealed, and %FALSE if you want the child widget to be hidden. |
| set_label | `Expander.set_label(label: utf8?) -> none` | gtk_expander_set_label |  | Sets the text of the label of the expander to @label. This will also clear any previously set labels. |

| set_use_markup | `Expander.set_use_markup(use_markup: gboolean) -> none` | gtk_expander_set_use_markup |  | Sets whether the text of the label contains Pango markup. |
| set_use_underline | `Expander.set_use_underline(use_underline: gboolean) -> none` | gtk_expander_set_use_underline |  | If true, an underline in the text indicates a mnemonic. |

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |

| label | utf8 | read, write | The text of the expanders label. |
| label-widget | Widget | read, write | A widget to display instead of the usual expander label. |
| resize-toplevel | gboolean | read, write | When this property is %TRUE, the expander will resize the toplevel widget containing the expander upon expanding and collapsing. |
| use-markup | gboolean | read, write | Whether the text in the label is Pango markup. |
| use-underline | gboolean | read, write | Whether an underline in the text indicates a mnemonic. |

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate | `` | none | last |  | Activates the `GtkExpander`. |

| set_wrap | `SpinButton.set_wrap(wrap: gboolean) -> none` | gtk_spin_button_set_wrap |  | Sets the flag that determines if a spin button value wraps around to the opposite limit when the upper or lower limit of the range is exceeded. |
| spin | `SpinButton.spin(direction: SpinType, increment: gdouble) -> none` | gtk_spin_button_spin |  | Increment or decrement a spin button???s value in a specified direction by a specified amount. |
| update | `SpinButton.update() -> none` | gtk_spin_button_update |  | Manually force an update of the spin button. |

#### Properties

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activates-default | gboolean | read, write | Whether to activate the default widget when the spin button is activated. See [signal@Gtk.SpinButton::activate] for what counts as activation. |
| adjustment | Adjustment | read, write | The adjustment that holds the value of the spin button. |
| climb-rate | gdouble | read, write | The acceleration rate when you hold down a button or key. |
| digits | guint | read, write | The number of decimal places to display. |
| numeric | gboolean | read, write | Whether non-numeric characters should be ignored. |
| snap-to-ticks | gboolean | read, write | Whether erroneous values are automatically changed to the spin buttons nearest step increment. |
| update-policy | SpinButtonUpdatePolicy | read, write | Whether the spin button should update always, or only when the value is acceptable. |
| value | gdouble | read, write | The current value. |
| wrap | gboolean | read, write | Whether a spin button should wrap upon reaching its limits. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| change-value | `scroll: ScrollType` | none | last |  | Emitted when the user initiates a value change. This is a keybinding signal. Applications should not connect to it, but may emit it with g_signal_e... |
| input | `new_value: out gdouble` | gint | last |  | Emitted to convert the users input into a double value. The signal handler is expected to use [method@Gtk.Editable.get_text] to retrieve the text o... |
| output | `` | gboolean | last |  | Emitted to tweak the formatting of the value for display. ```c // show leading zeros static gboolean on_output (GtkSpinButton *spin, gpointer data)... |
| value-changed | `` | none | last |  | Emitted when the value is changed. Also see the [signal@Gtk.SpinButton::output] signal. |
| wrapped | `` | none | last |  | Emitted right after the spinbutton wraps from its maximum to its minimum value or vice-versa. |

| toggle-overwrite | `` | none | last |  | Emitted to toggle the overwrite mode of the `GtkText`. This is a keybinding signal. The default bindings for this signal is <kbd>Insert</kbd>. |

#### Properties

| Name | Type | Access | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| can-redo | gboolean | read | Denotes that the buffer can reapply the last undone action. |
| can-undo | gboolean | read | Denotes that the buffer can undo the last applied action. |
| cursor-position | gint | read | The position of the insert mark. This is an offset from the beginning of the buffer. It is useful for getting notified when the cursor moves. |
| enable-undo | gboolean | read, write | Denotes if support for undoing and redoing changes to the buffer is allowed. |
| has-selection | gboolean | read | Whether the buffer has some text currently selected. |
| tag-table | TextTagTable | read, write, construct-only | The GtkTextTagTable for the buffer. |
| text | utf8 | read, write | The text content of the buffer. Without child widgets and images, see [method@Gtk.TextBuffer.get_text] for more information. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| columns-changed | `` | none | last |  | The number of columns of the treeview has changed. |
| cursor-changed | `` | none | last |  | The position of the cursor (focused cell) has changed. |
| expand-collapse-cursor-row | `object: gboolean, p0: gboolean, p1: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| move-cursor | `step: MovementStep, direction: gint, extend: gboolean, modify: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  | The `GtkTreeView`::move-cursor signal is a [keybinding signal][class@Gtk.SignalAction] which gets emitted when the user presses one of the cursor k... |
| row-activated | `path: TreePath, column: TreeViewColumn?` | none | last |  | The "row-activated" signal is emitted when the method [method@Gtk.TreeView.row_activated] is called. This signal is emitted when the user double-cl... |
| row-collapsed | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | none | last |  | The given row has been collapsed (child nodes are hidden). |
| row-expanded | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | none | last |  | The given row has been expanded (child nodes are shown). |
| select-all | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| select-cursor-parent | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| select-cursor-row | `object: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| start-interactive-search | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| test-collapse-row | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | gboolean | last |  | The given row is about to be collapsed (hide its children nodes). Use this signal if you need to control the collapsibility of individual rows. |
| test-expand-row | `iter: TreeIter, path: TreePath` | gboolean | last |  | The given row is about to be expanded (show its children nodes). Use this signal if you need to control the expandability of individual rows. |
| toggle-cursor-row | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |
| unselect-all | `` | gboolean | last |  |  |

| display | Gdk.Display | read, write | The display that will display this window. |
| focus-visible | gboolean | read, write | Whether 'focus rectangles' are currently visible in this window. This property is maintained by GTK based on user input and should not be set by ap... |
| focus-widget | Widget | read, write | The focus widget. |
| fullscreened | gboolean | read, write | Whether the window is fullscreen. Setting this property is the equivalent of calling [method@Gtk.Window.fullscreen] or [method@Gtk.Window.unfullscr... |
| handle-menubar-accel | gboolean | read, write | Whether the window frame should handle F10 for activating menubars. |
| hide-on-close | gboolean | read, write | If this window should be hidden when the users clicks the close button. |
| icon-name | utf8 | read, write | Specifies the name of the themed icon to use as the window icon. See [class@Gtk.IconTheme] for more details. |
| is-active | gboolean | read | Whether the toplevel is the currently active window. |
| maximized | gboolean | read, write | Whether the window is maximized. Setting this property is the equivalent of calling [method@Gtk.Window.maximize] or [method@Gtk.Window.unmaximize];... |
| mnemonics-visible | gboolean | read, write | Whether mnemonics are currently visible in this window. This property is maintained by GTK based on user input, and should not be set by applications. |
| modal | gboolean | read, write | If %TRUE, the window is modal. |
| resizable | gboolean | read, write | If %TRUE, users can resize the window. |
| startup-id | utf8 | write | A write-only property for setting window's startup notification identifier. |
| suspended | gboolean | read | Whether the window is suspended. See [method@Gtk.Window.is_suspended] for details about what suspended means. |
| title | utf8 | read, write | The title of the window. |
| titlebar | Widget | read, write | The titlebar widget. |
| transient-for | Window | read, write | The transient parent of the window. |

#### Signals

Connect in SQGI with `instance.connect("signal-name", handler)`.

| Signal | Handler Arguments | Returns | When | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate-default | `` | none | last |  | Emitted when the user activates the default widget of @window. This is a keybinding signal. |
| activate-focus | `` | none | last |  | Emitted when the user activates the currently focused widget of @window. This is a keybinding signal. |
| close-request | `` | gboolean | last |  | Emitted when the user clicks on the close button of the window. |
| enable-debugging | `toggle: gboolean` | gboolean | last |  | Emitted when the user enables or disables interactive debugging. When @toggle is %TRUE, interactive debugging is toggled on or off, when it is %FAL... |
| keys-changed | `` | none | first |  | emitted when the set of accelerators or mnemonics that are associated with @window changes. |

#### Virtual Methods

These are the names to use from `sqgi.register_class({ overrides = ... })` when SQGI can override them.

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| activate_default | `activate_default() -> none` |  |  | Activates the default widget for the window. |
| activate_focus | `activate_focus() -> none` |  |  | Activates the current focused widget within the window. |
| close_request | `close_request() -> gboolean` |  |  | Class handler for the `GtkWindow::close-request` signal. |
| enable_debugging | `enable_debugging(toggle: gboolean) -> gboolean` |  |  | Class handler for the `GtkWindow::enable-debugging` keybinding signal. |
| keys_changed | `keys_changed() -> none` |  |  | Signal gets emitted when the set of accelerators or mnemonics that are associated with window changes. |

### Gtk.AccessibleAutocomplete

GType: `GtkAccessibleAutocomplete` ?? C type: `GtkAccessibleAutocomplete`

The possible values for the %GTK_ACCESSIBLE_PROPERTY_AUTOCOMPLETE accessible property.

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| both | 3 | both | When a user is providing input, an element containing a collection of values that could complete the provided input may be displayed. If displayed,... |
| inline | 1 | inline | When a user is providing input, text suggesting one way to complete the provided input may be dynamically inserted after the caret. |
| list | 2 | list | When a user is providing input, an element containing a collection of values that could complete the provided input may be displayed. |
| none | 0 | none | Automatic suggestions are not displayed. |

### Gtk.AccessibleInvalidState

GType: `GtkAccessibleInvalidState` ?? C type: `GtkAccessibleInvalidState`

The possible values for the %GTK_ACCESSIBLE_STATE_INVALID accessible state. Note that the %GTK_ACCESSIBLE_INVALID_FALSE and %GTK_ACCESSIBLE_INVALID...

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| false | 0 | false | There are no detected errors in the value |
| grammar | 2 | grammar | A grammatical error was detected |
| spelling | 3 | spelling | A spelling error was detected |
| true | 1 | true | The value entered by the user has failed validation |

### Gtk.AccessiblePlatformState

GType: `GtkAccessiblePlatformState` ?? C type: `GtkAccessiblePlatformState`

The various platform states which can be queried using [method@Gtk.Accessible.get_platform_state].

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| active | 2 | active | whether the accessible is active |
| focusable | 0 | focusable | whether the accessible can be focused |
| focused | 1 | focused | whether the accessible has focus |

### Gtk.AccessibleProperty

GType: `GtkAccessibleProperty` ?? C type: `GtkAccessibleProperty`

The possible accessible properties of a [iface@Accessible].

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| autocomplete | 0 | autocomplete | Indicates whether inputting text could trigger display of one or more predictions of the user's intended value for a combobox, searchbox, or textbo... |
| description | 1 | description | Defines a string value that describes or annotates the current element. Value type: string |
| has_popup | 2 | has-popup | Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element, such as menu or dialog, that can be triggered by an element. |
| key_shortcuts | 3 | key-shortcuts | Indicates keyboard shortcuts that an author has implemented to activate or give focus to an element. Value type: string |
| label | 4 | label | Defines a string value that labels the current element. Value type: string |
| level | 5 | level | Defines the hierarchical level of an element within a structure. Value type: integer |
| modal | 6 | modal | Indicates whether an element is modal when displayed. Value type: boolean |
| multi_line | 7 | multi-line | Indicates whether a text box accepts multiple lines of input or only a single line. Value type: boolean |

| words | 2 | words | Delete words. |

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |

| external | 3 | external | Don't show a scrollbar, but don't force the size to follow the content. This can be used e.g. to make multiple scrolled windows share a scrollbar. |
| never | 2 | never | The scrollbar should never appear. In this mode the content determines the size. |

| initial | 0 | initial | The printing has not started yet; this status is set initially, and while the print dialog is shown. |
| pending | 4 | pending | The print job has been sent to the printer, but is not printed for some reason, e.g. the printer may be stopped. |
| pending_issue | 5 | pending-issue | Some problem has occurred during printing, e.g. a paper jam. |
| preparing | 1 | preparing | This status is set while the begin-print signal is emitted and during pagination. |
| printing | 6 | printing | The printer is processing the print job. |
| sending_data | 3 | sending-data | The print job is being sent off to the printer. |

GType: `GtkStackTransitionType` ?? C type: `GtkStackTransitionType`

#### Members

| Name | Value | Nick | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| crossfade | 1 | crossfade | A cross-fade |
| none | 0 | none | No transition |
| over_down | 9 | over-down | Cover the old page by sliding down |
| over_down_up | 17 | over-down-up | Cover the old page sliding down or uncover the new page sliding up, according to order |
| over_left | 10 | over-left | Cover the old page by sliding to the left |
| over_left_right | 18 | over-left-right | Cover the old page sliding left or uncover the new page sliding right, according to order |
| over_right | 11 | over-right | Cover the old page by sliding to the right |
| over_right_left | 19 | over-right-left | Cover the old page sliding right or uncover the new page sliding left, according to order |
| over_up | 8 | over-up | Cover the old page by sliding up |
| over_up_down | 16 | over-up-down | Cover the old page sliding up or uncover the new page sliding down, according to order |
| rotate_left | 20 | rotate-left | Pretend the pages are sides of a cube and rotate that cube to the left |
| rotate_left_right | 22 | rotate-left-right | Pretend the pages are sides of a cube and rotate that cube to the left or right according to the children order |
| rotate_right | 21 | rotate-right | Pretend the pages are sides of a cube and rotate that cube to the right |
| slide_down | 5 | slide-down | Slide from top down |
| slide_left | 3 | slide-left | Slide from right to left |
| slide_left_right | 6 | slide-left-right | Slide from left or right according to the children order |
| slide_right | 2 | slide-right | Slide from left to right |
| slide_up | 4 | slide-up | Slide from bottom up |
| slide_up_down | 7 | slide-up-down | Slide from top down or bottom up according to the order |
| under_down | 13 | under-down | Uncover the new page by sliding down |
| under_left | 14 | under-left | Uncover the new page by sliding to the left |
| under_right | 15 | under-right | Uncover the new page by sliding to the right |
| under_up | 12 | under-up | Uncover the new page by sliding up |

| to_string | `CssSection.to_string() -> utf8` | gtk_css_section_to_string |  | Prints the section into a human-readable text form using [method@Gtk.CssSection.print]. |
| unref | `CssSection.unref() -> none` | gtk_css_section_unref |  | Decrements the reference count on `section`, freeing the structure if the reference count reaches 0. |

### Gtk.ExpressionWatch

GType: `GtkExpressionWatch` ?? C type: `GtkExpressionWatch`

An opaque structure representing a watched `GtkExpression`. The contents of `GtkExpressionWatch` should only be accessed through the provided API.

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| evaluate | `ExpressionWatch.evaluate(value: GObject.Value) -> gboolean` | gtk_expression_watch_evaluate |  | Evaluates the watched expression and on success stores the result in `value`. This is equivalent to calling [method@Gtk.Expression.evaluate] with t... |
| ref | `ExpressionWatch.ref() -> ExpressionWatch` | gtk_expression_watch_ref |  | Acquires a reference on the given `GtkExpressionWatch`. |
| unref | `ExpressionWatch.unref() -> none` | gtk_expression_watch_unref |  | Releases a reference on the given `GtkExpressionWatch`. If the reference was the last, the resources associated to `self` are freed. |
| unwatch | `ExpressionWatch.unwatch() -> none` | gtk_expression_watch_unwatch |  | Stops watching an expression. See [method@Gtk.Expression.watch] for how the watch was established. |

### Gtk.PaperSize

GType: `GtkPaperSize` ?? C type: `GtkPaperSize`

`GtkPaperSize` handles paper sizes. It uses the standard called PWG 5101.1-2002 PWG: Standard for Media Standardized Names to name the paper sizes ...

#### Constructors

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| new | `PaperSize.new(name: utf8?) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new |  | Creates a new `GtkPaperSize` object by parsing a PWG 5101.1-2002 paper name. If @name is %NULL, the default paper size is returned, see [func@Gtk.P... |
| new_custom | `PaperSize.new_custom(name: utf8, display_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble, unit: Unit) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_custom |  | Creates a new `GtkPaperSize` object with the given parameters. |
| new_from_gvariant | `PaperSize.new_from_gvariant(variant: GLib.Variant) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_gvariant |  | Deserialize a paper size from a `GVariant`. The `GVariant must be in the format produced by [method@Gtk.PaperSize.to_gvariant]. |
| new_from_ipp | `PaperSize.new_from_ipp(ipp_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_ipp |  | Creates a new `GtkPaperSize` object by using IPP information. If @ipp_name is not a recognized paper name, @width and @height are used to construct... |
| new_from_key_file | `PaperSize.new_from_key_file(key_file: GLib.KeyFile, group_name: utf8?) -> PaperSize throws` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_key_file |  | Reads a paper size from the group @group_name in the key file @key_file. |
| new_from_ppd | `PaperSize.new_from_ppd(ppd_name: utf8, ppd_display_name: utf8, width: gdouble, height: gdouble) -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_new_from_ppd |  | Creates a new `GtkPaperSize` object by using PPD information. If @ppd_name is not a recognized PPD paper name, @ppd_display_name, @width and @heigh... |

#### Static Functions

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| get_default | `get_default() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_default |  | Returns the name of the default paper size, which depends on the current locale. |
| get_paper_sizes | `get_paper_sizes(include_custom: gboolean) -> GLib.List` | gtk_paper_size_get_paper_sizes |  | Creates a list of known paper sizes. |

#### Methods

| Name | Signature | C symbol | Since | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| copy | `PaperSize.copy() -> PaperSize` | gtk_paper_size_copy |  | Copies an existing `GtkPaperSize`. |
| free | `PaperSize.free() -> none` | gtk_paper_size_free |  | Free the given `GtkPaperSize` object. |
| get_default_bottom_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_bottom_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_bottom_margin |  | Gets the default bottom margin for the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_default_left_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_left_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_left_margin |  | Gets the default left margin for the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_default_right_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_right_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_right_margin |  | Gets the default right margin for the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_default_top_margin | `PaperSize.get_default_top_margin(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_default_top_margin |  | Gets the default top margin for the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_display_name | `PaperSize.get_display_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_display_name |  | Gets the human-readable name of the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_height | `PaperSize.get_height(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_height |  | Gets the paper height of the `GtkPaperSize`, in units of @unit. |
| get_name | `PaperSize.get_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_name |  | Gets the name of the `GtkPaperSize`. |
| get_ppd_name | `PaperSize.get_ppd_name() -> utf8` | gtk_paper_size_get_ppd_name |  | Gets the PPD name of the `GtkPaperSize`, which may be %NULL. |
| get_width | `PaperSize.get_width(unit: Unit) -> gdouble` | gtk_paper_size_get_width |  | Gets the paper width of the `GtkPaperSize`, in units of @unit. |
| is_custom | `PaperSize.is_custom() -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_custom |  | Returns %TRUE if @size is not a standard paper size. |
| is_equal | `PaperSize.is_equal(size2: PaperSize) -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_equal |  | Compares two `GtkPaperSize` objects. |
| is_ipp | `PaperSize.is_ipp() -> gboolean` | gtk_paper_size_is_ipp |  | Returns %TRUE if @size is an IPP standard paper size. |
| set_size | `PaperSize.set_size(width: gdouble, height: gdouble, unit: Unit) -> none` | gtk_paper_size_set_size |  | Changes the dimensions of a @size to @width x @height. |
| to_gvariant | `PaperSize.to_gvariant() -> GLib.Variant` | gtk_paper_size_to_gvariant |  | Serialize a paper size to an `a{sv}` variant. |
| to_key_file | `PaperSize.to_key_file(key_file: GLib.KeyFile, group_name: utf8) -> none` | gtk_paper_size_to_key_file |  | This function adds the paper size from @size to @key_file. |
